title: Set `SameSite` attribute on cookies
issue_text:
This implements additional protection from some CSRF vulnerabilities.

Docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Guides/Cookies#controlling_third-party_cookies_with_samesite

### Testing done

None, <s>I expect PCT/ATH to fail with some SSO security realm plugins.</s> No tests failed 🎉

In manual testing https://plugins.jenkins.io/saml/ no longer redirects to the page you were on previously, as that was done through a session attribute read when processing `POST …/finishLogin`. This is being fixed by https://github.com/jenkinsci/saml-plugin/pull/514.

### Proposed changelog entries

- When running in Jetty (when using `java -jar jenkins.war`, the default), set the `SameSite` attribute for cookies to provide additional protection from some CSRF vulnerabilities. By default this sets the attribute to `Lax`. This can be changed by setting the Java system property `jenkins.security.JettySameSiteCookieSetup.sameSiteDefault`.
